text: This is the VOA Special English Technology Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语科技报道。

text: Smartphones, tablet computers, and e -readers have become a part of everyday life for many people.
zh: 智能手机、平板电脑和电子阅读器已经成为许多人日常生活的一部分。

text: They have also led to an explosion in the number of mobile applications.
zh: 它们也带动了移动应用程序数量的爆发式增长。

text: There is an app for just about anything you can think of.
zh: 几乎你能想到的任何东西都有相应的应用程序。

text: And more and more apps are being created every day.
zh: 而且每天都有越来越多的应用程序被开发出来。

text: Robert D 'Onozzi is a Hollywood movie producer.
zh: 罗伯特·唐纳齐是好莱坞的一名电影制片人。

text: He is perhaps best well -known as the executive producer of Flight Plan,
zh: 他或许最为人熟知的身份,是电影《航班蛇影》的执行制片人,

text: the 2005 movie starring actress Jodie Foster.
zh: 这部2005年的电影由女演员朱迪·福斯特主演。

text: His most recent claim to fame is as co -developer of a new app called VoiceCal.
zh: 他最近的成名之作,是作为一款名为VoiceCal的新应用程序的联合开发者。

text: It is a mobile calendar app that uses voice recognition software.
zh: 这是一款使用语音识别软件的移动日历应用。

text: Users are able to enter appointments into their iPhone calendar without having to type in the information.
zh: 用户无需手动输入信息,就能将日程安排添加到iPhone日历中。

text: He said although he has a mobile phone,
zh: 他说,尽管自己有手机,

text: he had to stop what he was doing to put things in his calendar. In his words,
zh: 却常常不得不停下手头的事情去记录日程。用他的话说,

text: It got to the point where I just wasn't using the calendar on my iPhone, and neither was anybody else that I knew.
zh: “事情严重到我根本不用iPhone上的日历了,我认识的其他人也是如此。”

text: Robert Dannazzi decided there had to be a better way.
zh: 罗伯特·唐纳齐认为一定有更好的办法。

text: So he teamed up with a friend who is a developer. The two
zh: 于是他与一位从事开发工作的朋友合作。两人

text: used voice recognition software from a company called Nuance to build VoiceCal.
zh: 使用一家名为Nuance公司的语音识别软件,开发出了VoiceCal。

text: He says when you use VoiceCal, you can say any event you want to put into your calendar
zh: 他说,使用VoiceCal时,你只需说出想加入日历的任何事项,

text: and it will appear there. If you have two or more events to add to the calendar,
zh: 它就会自动出现在日历里。如果你要添加两件或更多事项,

text: you separate them by using the word also.
zh: 只需用“也”这个词把它们分开即可。

text: Mr. Dannazzi says all of this can be done hands -free.
zh: 唐纳齐先生说,这一切都可以完全解放双手来完成。

text: In settings, you can make it fully automatic,
zh: “在设置中,你可以让它完全自动运行,

text: so I don't have to touch anything, he says. Robert Dannazzi
zh: 这样我就什么都不用碰了,”他说。罗伯特·唐纳齐

text: says there is a great need for an app like VoiceCal among busy businessmen like himself.
zh: 表示,像他这样忙碌的商务人士非常需要VoiceCal这样的应用程序。

text: He hopes the app will be successful over the long term.
zh: 他希望这款应用能够长期获得成功。

text: He says, in the movie business, you can do all the advertising in the world,
zh: 他说,在电影行业,你可以投入天价的广告宣传,

text: and that can get you a great opening weekend.
zh: 这能让你获得出色的首映周末票房。

text: But if the movie's not good, it will die off.
zh: 但如果电影本身不够好,它终将逐渐消失。

text: So really, what supports a film, just like what supports an app, is word of mouth.
zh: 所以说,真正支撑一部电影的,就像支撑一款应用一样,其实是口碑。

text: Matt Kozlov is the head of a startup technology company
zh: 马特·科兹洛夫是一家科技创业公司的负责人,

text: called Moonshark in Los Angeles, California.
zh: 这家公司名为Moonshark,位于美国加利福尼亚州洛杉矶。

text: The company makes game apps for mobile devices.
zh: 该公司为移动设备制作游戏应用程序。

text: It's an exploding marketplace right now, he says.
zh: 他说,这是一个正在爆发式增长的市场。

text: There are hundreds of millions of devices out there right now that we can reach.
zh: “目前市面上有数以亿计的设备,我们都能够触及到。”

text: Jennifer Lopez is helping Moonshark with a finger breakdancing game called Dance Pad.
zh: 詹妮弗·洛佩兹正在帮助Moonshark开发一款名为Dance Pad的手指霹雳舞游戏。

text: The company is also working with film director John Woo
zh: 该公司还与电影导演吴宇森合作,

text: on an action game expected to be released at the end of this year.
zh: 开发一款预计于今年年底发布的动作游戏。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Alex Villarreal.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语,我是亚历克斯·维拉里尔。
